2026-03-31 20:14:01
“Heated Rivalry” received 3 nominations at @TheWebbyAwards! The 2026 winners will be announced April 21st and recognised on May 11th. Vote via the link next to each category below. “Heated Rivalry” • Television & Film (General Social) https://bit.ly/4uTvY3A • Entertainment (General Social) https://bit.ly/3QcBLBi ‘Heated Rivalry’: Hudson Williams vs. Connor Storrie – Razorfish • Best Community or Fan Engagement: https://bit.ly/4lZr6G2 The awards recognise achievements across podcasts, AI, websites, creators, video & film, social media, games, apps, software and more.

original tweet

2026-03-31 19:24:10
‘Heated Rivalry’ costume designer Hanna Puley on Shane Hollander’s Canada fleece, and the intentional juxtaposition with Ilya Rozanov’s long black coat “I wanted something that was very comfortable, that wouldn’t draw too much attention to Shane, because it’s not something he would have worn if it was too eye-catching. He needed something that would be a little more muted and understated. (…) For the Olympics, there’s a lot of design that sometimes feels a little extra, it’s hyper-designed in some ways. So I wanted to pare it down.” “Shane in a fleece gives such a good contrast to Ilya in this long black coat in the stands. That was kind of the priority for me.” The visual language is obvious once she says it aloud. Shane is soft, safe, familiar. Ilya is harder, sharper, more difficult to ignore. “Shane represents something that feels very comfortable, he’s not trying too hard. He’s just doing something that feels very, very safe.” 🔗 Read more here: https://elle.in/fashion/in-conversation-with-hanna-puley-the-costume-designer-behind-heated-rivalry-11439819

2026-03-28 20:23:16
Ethan Wingrove talks about working with Hudson Williams for “Sheepskin”, and wanting to cast him as a ‘cold blooded’ character at NFFTY Film Festival in Seattle today (via proseandpucks) “He was like ‘just let me produce it man, I’ll give you some money for it’, because he wanted to motivate me to [make it]. And it ended up being a really collaborative environment on set.”

2026-03-27

2026-03-27 20:37:23
Hudson Williams’ upcoming projects 🎬 ‘Rancid’ (2026) 🎬 ‘Encore’ (2026) 🎬 ‘Sheepskin’ (2026) 🎬 ‘Heated Rivalry’ Season 2 (Crave, 2027) 🎬 ‘The Altruists’ series (Netflix, TBA) 🎬 ‘Yaga’ series (Crave, TBA) 🎬 ‘Apparatus’ (TBA) 🎬 ‘8’ (TBA) 🎬 ‘Yoko Loko’ (TBA) More information: https://tinyurl.com/2n6mr3y2

original tweet

2026-03-27 20:31:14
🎬 HUDSON WILLIAMS’ UPCOMING PROJECTS 🎬 ✧ RANCID (2026), as Jacob, also co-writer, co-director and co-producer A short film by Hudson Williams and Alex Vance. Hollywood ShortsFest 2026 Official Selection. (Source: https://hollywoodshortsfest.com/lineup/) ✧ ENCORE (2026), as Drifter (Mason) Following his classmate’s monologue confessing to murder, Victor wrestles with the unsettling possibility that it may have been more than just a performance. Feature film currently in post-production. (Trailer: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt34189825/) ✧ SHEEPSKIN (2026), as Blue, also producer Directed and written by Ethan Wingrove, 3 young criminals are being interrogated to reveal the location of their mysterious boss. But what they don’t reveal, is even more deadly. Short film releasing Winter 2026. (Trailer: https://youtu.be/UfGVLjsJUhs) ✧ HEATED RIVALRY, Season 2 (2027), as Shane Hollander Based on the Game Changers novels by author Rachel Reid, ‘Heated Rivalry’ follows the years-long relationship between rivals-turned-lovers Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov (Connor Storrie), star hockey payers for Montreal and Boston, respectively. Their secret relationship developed over the course of the six-episode first season, starting as a friends-with-benefits situation before turning into a full-blown love affair. The second season will be following the storyline of ‘The Long Game’. (Source: https://deadline.com/2025/12/heated-rivalry-season-2-crave-hbo-max-1236646802/) ✧ THE ALTRUISTS (TBA), as Duncan Rheingans Yoo Follows the tumultuous romance between FTX co-founder Sam Bankman-Fried and his girlfriend Caroline Ellison. It depicts their ambitious attempts to revolutionize the financial system, leading to a $36 billion fraud. Netflix’s limited series is currently in post-production. (Source: https://deadline.com/2026/03/hudson-williams-jennifer-grey-cast-the-altruists-1236757124/) ✧ APPARATUS (TBA), as Tyler ‘Apparatus’ follows struggling young rideshare driver Tyler as he falls under the influence of a self-styled entrepreneur, JP (Dylan O’Brien), who offers him a future in his handheld massager business. But what appears to be a dream opportunity unravels as JP pulls Tyler into a violent downward spiral. (Source: https://deadline.com/2026/03/hudson-williams-dylan-obrien-to-star-apparatus-thriller-1236768004/) ✧ YAGA (TBA), as Henry Park Crave’s debut half-hour drama series ‘Yaga’ is about the myth of Baba Yaga. Henry Park is the young heir to a powerful fishery. Rapp (Noah Reid), a private investigator, is probing his disapperance and finds himself at odds with an apprehensive local detective, Carson (Clark Backo), a charismatic university professor with a taste for younger men, Katherine (Carrie-Anne Moss), and a labyrinth of enigmatic suspects, secret lives, and ancient magic. (Source: https://deadline.com/2026/02/heated-rivalry-hudson-williams-crave-yaga-drama-series-1236728206/) ✧ YOKO LOKO (TBA), as one of the main characters, also director and producer (Source (8:24): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pKpeGdbkzXw&t=524s) ✧ 8 (TBA), as Darius Set in 2005, a terminally-ill man embarks on an escapist road trip to Osoyoos with his unsuspecting pregnant wife. Unbeknownst to them, his influence on strangers during this time shapes their daughter’s life through the workings of karma. Feature film in post-production. (Source: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt23897276/)

original tweet

2026-03-27 18:54:28
Hudson Williams and Dylan O’Brien will star in ‘Apparatus’, a darkly comedic thriller marking the feature directorial debut of Canadian actress Sofia Banzhaf (via @DEADLINE) “‘Apparatus’ follows struggling young rideshare driver Tyler (Williams) as he falls under the influence of a self-styled entrepreneur, JP (O’Brien), who offers him a future in his handheld massager business. But what appears to be a dream opportunity unravels as JP pulls Tyler into a violent downward spiral.” Production dates and locations are still being finalized. 🔗 More information: https://deadline.com/2026/03/hudson-williams-dylan-obrien-to-star-apparatus-thriller-1236768004/

original tweet

2026-03-27 18:18:47
Aika Flores talks to @adam_hurly on Blueprint Grooming about working with Hudson Williams over the last few months On meeting Hudson for his Timid Magazine photoshoot: “He was, as you can imagine, just like a regular dude. He was super open and like the creative he's always been, he's still to this day, like really wants to be involved in the creative. We had a lot of fun that day.” On how he treats his team like a family: “He really treats his team like family and we're a family. So I think every person that he brings onto his team has a special purpose, not just the job, but I think in his life. Yeah, that has a special role in his life and mentally, emotionally sometimes, like physically because he does love a good hug.” Her top 3 Hudson hairstyles: 1. Balenciaga campaign 2. Oscars 3. Balenciaga Paris fashion show She also discusses his Wonderland shoot, his The Cut skincare video, working on “Heated Rivalry” press junkets, his Oscar fan, and more. 🔗 Watch here: https://youtu.be/JieCa1prutA

original tweet

2026-03-27 16:20:14
Happy Birthday Sophie ❤️ Hudson is so lucky to have you! Your whimsy energy and happiness has brought so much joy to this friendship and we can’t wait to see more of you two together 🫶🏻🤏🏻

original tweet

2026-03-27 15:29:50
Lola Tung talks about dancing with Hudson Williams at the Gold House Lunar New Year celebration last month to @Variety “There’s something really special I think, because I met Hudson and also Yerin Ha at that party... it feels like when you have a similar experience where, Hudson’s experience, I can’t even imagine that... It’s that thing where you instantly have this connection of like, ‘Hi, are you okay? Like, how are you feeling? Do you need anything? Do you want to just dance and have a good time?’, (...) and he was like ‘Yeah, let’s dance!’ We get each other. (...) I do appreciate that he was like, ‘Let’s just have a good time.’” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/GBA_3x-uV5A

original tweet

2026-03-27 03:23:54

original tweet

2026-03-27 03:23:51
Jacob Tierney revealed that Season 2 is already fully outlined 👀 He also shared during Stage32 Q&A that there’s no much improvisation, that the show is very scripted, with a clear vision from the start, though he still encourages the actors to experiment a bit with the material and blocking. Talking about episode 6, he said he was surprised by how everything turned out especially considering all the cottage scenes were filmed in just two days, with only the two actors carrying the entire weight of it with the crew. He didn’t realize how risky that was until they were actually filming. Source: https://www.threads.com/@dawn_serra/post/DWSPUOLgbzB?xmt=AQF0C8QAyrn_fDqOx58gPZPZ0Gie4P7zbJbY3VCVVUDx4AvuecOi_ZJl6CJmCFQoZOIl-FZu&slof=1 https://www.threads.com/@dawn_serra/post/DWSOMhlgYSp?xmt=AQF0E08juyw0Bl5kVk_UGAO2a0rOcPoN_ZJuVJUEABDCyzk2eB3fNrFMXBhPaNQ67cok_PY&slof=1 https://www.threads.com/@dawn_serra/post/DWSJzz8kpw-?xmt=AQF0HQo3pZik2yRRpWtqwisXOj3_wq-V3SVClkm4vY0ZpCwZyXPDThx08IZZ7fWU151kBBwe&slof=1

original tweet

2026-03-26 16:03:24
Dylan O'Brien and Hudson Williams are set to star in the upcoming thriller 'Apparatus,' which begins filming in Toronto this spring. The film follows a struggling ride-share driver who gets pulled into a dangerous spiral after crossing paths with a charismatic entrepreneur offering what seems like a dream opportunity. Filming is scheduled to run from March 30 through May 12, with Sofia Banzhaf directing and co-writing the project. Source: https://www.instagram.com/p/DWWctJKDg7u/?igsh=NTc4MTIwNjQ2YQ==

“I have. I’ve seen the first episode and I thought that it was really good. It’s really, really good, really well written. [It has] great actors; that’s a great show.” - Former American footballer Tom Brady when asked if he has watched “Heated Rivalry” (via @THR) https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/lifestyle/lifestyle-news/tom-brady-ferrero-world-cup-soccer-contest-campaign-details-1236547243/

original tweet

2026-03-26 12:09:04
Costume designer Hanna Puley talks to @TheNodMag about working on “Heated Rivalry” season 1, and her ideas for season 2 “When the series returns for the second season, Puley suspects the characters’ wardrobes may begin to evolve along with their relationship. After all, couples inevitably influence or mirror each other’s style. ‘Maybe Shane and Ilya will start dressing more alike’, she says. ‘Clothing is such a marker of your environment, your community, and how you see yourself. When I was designing for those characters, I liked the idea of each of them having someone: Kip has Elena, Ilya has Svetlana, and Shane has Rose.’” “Will they keep Shane’s stylist? ‘Maybe Ilya would be like ‘You don’t need a stylist if you’ve got me’. I don’t think Ilya needs a stylist. He’s doing okay’, she laughs.” 🔗 Read more here: https://thenodmag.com/content/heated-rivalry-tv-series-costume-designer-hanna-puley-interview

2026-03-25 21:59:35
Virgin Radio Victoria will be offering Hudson Williams-inspired bumper stickers tomorrow, March 26, at 503 Park Place (Victoria, BC) between 7 AM and 10 AM! Suggested donation between $5-$10 per sticker. All proceeds will go to the nonprofit Victoria Humane Society. They will have four designs to choose from: - “Boring like Hollander” - “Dead as F***, Old People” - “Victoria: a stabilizing place to stay” - “Dead AF and we like it that way” 🔗 https://www.instagram.com/p/DWUIEUNAbTR/

original tweet

2026-03-25 20:39:56
Hudson Williams has influenced a surge in applications to Langara College’s Film Arts Production Programme, the department head reveals The program is split into three streams; writing, directing and acting - Jonas Quastel says the acting stream has triple the applications of the others: “We should have some really great actors next year.” “Jonas, who taught Hudson before he graduated in 2020, says he always expected him to succeed. (…) With the rise in popularity of the program, students are excited at the talent it might attract.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.langaravoice.ca/the-hudson-effect-heated-enrolment-for-langaras-film-arts-program/

“Heated Rivalry” received 18 nominations at the 2026 Canadian Screen Awards, including ‘Best Drama Series’! ‘Best Direction (Drama Series) ‘Best Writing (Drama Series)’ ‘Best Photography (Drama)’ ‘Best Picture Editing (Drama, x2)’ ‘Best Sound (Fiction)’ ‘Best Costume Design’ ‘Best Visual Effects’ ‘Best Original Music (Drama)’ ‘Best Original Music – Original Song - “It’s You”’ ‘Best Lead Performer (Drama Series, x2)’ ‘Best Supporting Performer (Drama, x2)’ ‘Best Guest Performance (Drama Series)’ ‘Best Achievement in Casting (Fiction)’ ‘Best Production Design or Art Direction (Fiction)’ 🔗 http://academy.ca/nominees/

“Heated Rivalry” received 18 nominations at the 2026 Canadian Screen Awards, including ‘Best Drama Series’! ‘Best Direction (Drama Series) ‘Best Writing (Drama Series)’ ‘Best Photography (Drama)’ ‘Best Picture Editing (Drama, x2)’ ‘Best Sound (Fiction)’ ‘Best Costume Design’ ‘Best Visual Effects’ ‘Best Original Music (Drama)’ ‘Best Original Music – Original Song - “It’s You”’ ‘Best Lead Performer (Drama Series, x2)’ ‘Best Supporting Performer (Drama, x2)’ ‘Best Guest Performance (Drama Series)’ ‘Best Achievement in Casting (Fiction)’ ‘Best Production Design or Art Direction (Fiction)’ 🔗 http://academy.ca/nominees/

2026-03-24 18:08:43
Rachel Reid via Instagram 🤍 “I know there have been a LOT of images/videos/interviews etc of, y’know, all of us, and this one is very old now, but listen: I wish I could coherently explain how I feel when I look at this image. I want that for every author. Like, it STILL doesn’t feel like it can possibly be real. I wish I could show my 5-years-ago self this image. It would make her brain melt.”

original tweet

2026-03-24 16:24:58
The “Heated Rivalry” album will feature a selection of songs heard in the show (from the likes of Harrison, t.A.T.u., Wet Leg, Wolf Parade) alongside Peter Peter’s original score. Pre-orders for the album — which will be released via Milan Records on CD, vinyl and as a digital download — start on March 26. Both editions will contain a fold-out poster, imagery from the series & liner notes from show creator Jacob Tierney. Sign up: https://heatedrivalry.lnk.to/pre-order Source: https://www.billboard.com/music/music-news/heated-rivalry-soundtrack-cd-vinyl-release-1236205225/ https://milanrecords.com/faq-a-quick-guide-to-the-heated-rivalry-soundtrack-vinyl-cd-pre-order/

original tweet

Pre-orders for the album — which will be released via Milan Records on CD, vinyl and as a digital download — start on March 26. Both editions will contain a fold-out poster, imagery from the series & liner notes from show creator Jacob Tierney. Pre-orders for the album — which will be released via Milan Records on CD, vinyl and as a digital download — start on March 26. Both editions will contain a fold-out poster, imagery from the series & liner notes from show creator Jacob Tierney. Sign up: https://heatedrivalry.lnk.to/pre-order Source: https://www.billboard.com/music/music-news/heated-rivalry-soundtrack-cd-vinyl-release-1236205225/ https://milanrecords.com/faq-a-quick-guide-to-the-heated-rivalry-soundtrack-vinyl-cd-pre-order/

original tweet

2026-03-24 14:41:50
Spoiler warning: Jacob Tierney on what he’s most excited to adapt from “The Long Game” for season 2 of “Heated Rivalry” (via @PinkNews) “I don’t have my full plan worked out yet. When Shane and Ilya get married, like by Hayden’s kids, it’s like the cutest thing I’ve ever seen where of course I’m going to do that. So there’s a thing I can commit to. I just don’t want to commit to… I want to wheel out a little room to manoeuvre. But that for sure will enter in. I mean, truly, the thing I’m looking forward to so much is writing Ilya and Hayden in season two because of how funny they are together and that endless antagonism that I think will be very, very charming.” 🔗 https://www.thepinknews.com/2026/03/24/heated-rivalrys-jacob-tierney-talks-season-two-stigma-and-happy-endings-our-stories-dont-have-to-be-traumatic/

original tweet

When Shane and Ilya get married, like by Hayden’s kids, it’s like the cutest thing I’ve ever seen where of course I’m going to do that. So there’s a thing I can commit to. I just don’t want to commit to… I want to wheel out a little room to manoeuvre. But that for sure will enter in. I mean, truly, the thing I’m looking forward to so much is writing Ilya and Hayden in season two because of how funny they are together and that endless antagonism that I think will be very, very charming.” 🔗 https://www.thepinknews.com/2026/03/24/heated-rivalrys-jacob-tierney-talks-season-two-stigma-and-happy-endings-our-stories-dont-have-to-be-traumatic/

2026-03-23 20:00:59
Quinn ‘Ember & Ice’ intimacy coordinator Jamie Monahan on Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie’s professional approach to recording intimacy (via Rational Romantics Podcast) CASS: “It seems like it would be such a fascinating development, especially when they have an existing, like Hudson and Connor have this existing relationship, existing rapport, to get to kind of tap into that. Seems like it would be a very incredible experience to have everything coordinated and set. But also, I mean, they're very clearly, very comfortable with each other. So that would help, I would think.” JAMIE: “I think what was beautiful that I picked up on very quickly was the language that they used in terms of intimacy and the questions that they asked. I could tell that they had worked with a really great intimacy coordinator. And Chala Hunter was the intimacy coordinator on Heated Rivalry and the language around intimacy, how we use intimacy to further plot development, character development, and what's happening in the story is something so powerful.” “And I think once actors understand that intimacy can be such a massive tool in terms of creativity, it's very freeing. And they both approached the work that way. And I could tell it was because they had worked with an intimacy coordinator before.” “So it was very easy because it was jumping into a shorthand with someone who already had that kind of experience, and we could go from there. So, yes, it was definitely very helpful that they had experiences together, but also having had worked with an intimacy coordinator before made it very easy for me not to have to go into the whole spiel of, ‘This is how we work together, this is what I do, this is why I'm here’, you know. We're already on the same page for everything, which was really nice.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/rational-romantics/id1797195703?i=1000756591484&r=1709

original tweet

2026-03-23 20:00:59
Quinn ‘Ember & Ice’ intimacy coordinator Jamie Monahan on Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie’s professional approach to recording intimacy (via Rational Romantics Podcast) CASS: “It seems like it would be such a fascinating development, especially when they have an existing, like Hudson and Connor have this existing relationship, existing rapport, to get to kind of tap into that. Seems like it would be a very incredible experience to have everything coordinated and set. But also, I mean, they're very clearly, very comfortable with each other. So that would help, I would think.” JAMIE: “I think what was beautiful that I picked up on very quickly was the language that they used in terms of intimacy and the questions that they asked. I could tell that they had worked with a really great intimacy coordinator. And Chala Hunter was the intimacy coordinator on Heated Rivalry and the language around intimacy, how we use intimacy to further plot development, character development, and what's happening in the story is something so powerful.” “And I think once actors understand that intimacy can be such a massive tool in terms of creativity, it's very freeing. And they both approached the work that way. And I could tell it was because they had worked with an intimacy coordinator before.” “So it was very easy because it was jumping into a shorthand with someone who already had that kind of experience, and we could go from there. So, yes, it was definitely very helpful that they had experiences together, but also having had worked with an intimacy coordinator before made it very easy for me not to have to go into the whole spiel of, ‘This is how we work together, this is what I do, this is why I'm here’, you know. We're already on the same page for everything, which was really nice.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/rational-romantics/id1797195703?i=1000756591484&r=1709

She wants to stay anonymous but thank you so much for your kindness 🥺)

2026-03-23 14:13:20
‘Rancid’ co-star Martina Vos talks about working with Hudson Williams and the popularity of the upcoming Hollywood ShortsFest screenings (via @stalbertgazette) “We were very fortunate. Our first show sold out within 10 minutes, most likely due to Hudson. They added two more shows, and they sold out as well.” Vos added that ‘Blue Valentine’ is one of Williams’ favourite films. “He wanted to make something similar and very intense. He’s known for comedy in the indie scene. This was his chance to go hard in a dramatic experience. There were some heavy emotional scenes, but it was never not fun. Hudson is very grounded. He has a subtle style. He has a lot behind his eyes. But when he does comedy, he’s balls to the wall. He’ll do anything. He absolutely has this wild brain.” 🔗 https://www.stalbertgazette.com/local-entertainment/st-albert-actress-heads-to-hollywood-shortsfest-12026064

original tweet

2026-03-23 14:13:20
‘Rancid’ co-star Martina Vos talks about working with Hudson Williams and the popularity of the upcoming Hollywood ShortsFest screenings (via @stalbertgazette) “We were very fortunate. Our first show sold out within 10 minutes, most likely due to Hudson. They added two more shows, and they sold out as well.” Vos added that ‘Blue Valentine’ is one of Williams’ favourite films. “He wanted to make something similar and very intense. He’s known for comedy in the indie scene. This was his chance to go hard in a dramatic experience. There were some heavy emotional scenes, but it was never not fun. Hudson is very grounded. He has a subtle style. He has a lot behind his eyes. But when he does comedy, he’s balls to the wall. He’ll do anything. Our first show sold out within 10 minutes, most likely due to Hudson. They added two more shows, and they sold out as well.” Vos added that ‘Blue Valentine’ is one of Williams’ favourite films. “He wanted to make something similar and very intense. He’s known for comedy in the indie scene. This was his chance to go hard in a dramatic experience. There were some heavy emotional scenes, but it was never not fun. Hudson is very grounded. He absolutely has this wild brain.” 🔗 https://www.stalbertgazette.com/local-entertainment/st-albert-actress-heads-to-hollywood-shortsfest-12026064

original tweet

2026-03-23 12:15:39
Rachel Reid discusses Shane Hollander’s autism in “Heated Rivalry”, and Hudson Williams’s strong understanding of this trait (via @PinkNews) “When I wrote the first book, it was more about his overall personality. But I’ve talked about before, how in the time between Heated Rivalry and The Long Game, my own child was getting assessed for things like ADHD and autism. I learned a lot over those few years, and then I was like, ‘Oh yeah, Shane is autistic.’ By the time I wrote The Long Game, I was like, yeah, that’s what it is.” “Jacob knew right away. Jacob told me I think in the very first Zoom call, he was like, ‘Well, Shane’s autistic.’ He’s definitely approached the character that way. And when I talked to Hudson about it, he said the same thing. He was like, ‘Well, Shane is very autistic.’ We’d have a discussion about something where Connor might say something about Shane and I heard Hudson be like, ‘No, no, [he’s] doing that because he’s autistic.’ And I was like, ‘Yes, yes.’” “I think because of that, which unfortunately is also common of people with autism, the things that Shane does and says get misunderstood, sometimes by characters in the book and sometimes by readers who are like, ‘Well, that was a terrible thing to say or do.’ And it’s like, well, he’s not great at reading social cues. He’s got a lot of internalised anxiety. He’s very particular about certain things. Things bother him that don’t bother other people. I’m glad that everybody involved with the character understands that and I didn’t even have to explain it. Jacob got it. Hudson got it. I think most readers get it too. Lately I’ve been trying to just make it clear that, yes, he’s an autistic character.” 🔗 https://www.thepinknews.com/2026/03/23/heated-rivalrys-rachel-reid-on-developing-shane-and-ilya-their-group-chat-and-queer-literature-ilya-changed-a-lot/

original tweet

2026-03-23 12:15:39
Rachel Reid discusses Shane Hollander’s autism in “Heated Rivalry”, and Hudson Williams’s strong understanding of this trait (via @PinkNews) “When I wrote the first book, it was more about his overall personality. But I’ve talked about before, how in the time between Heated Rivalry and The Long Game, my own child was getting assessed for things like ADHD and autism. I learned a lot over those few years, and then I was like, ‘Oh yeah, Shane is autistic.’ By the time I wrote The Long Game, I was like, yeah, that’s what it is.” “Jacob knew right away. Jacob told me I think in the very first Zoom call, he was like, ‘Well, Shane’s autistic.’ He’s definitely approached the character that way. And when I talked to Hudson about it, he said the same thing. He was like, ‘Well, Shane is very autistic.’ We’d have a discussion about something where Connor might say something about Shane and I heard Hudson be like, ‘No, no, [he’s] doing that because he’s autistic.’ And I was like, ‘Yes, yes.’” “I think because of that, which unfortunately is also common of people with autism, the things that Shane does and says get misunderstood, sometimes by characters in the book and sometimes by readers who are like, ‘Well, that was a terrible thing to say or do.’ And it’s like, well, he’s not great at reading social cues. He’s got a lot of internalised anxiety. He’s very particular about certain things. Things bother him that don’t bother other people. I’m glad that everybody involved with the character understands that and I didn’t even have to explain it. Jacob got it. Hudson got it. I think most readers get it too. Lately I’ve been trying to just make it clear that, yes, he’s an autistic character.” 🔗 https://www.thepinknews.com/2026/03/23/heated-rivalrys-rachel-reid-on-developing-shane-and-ilya-their-group-chat-and-queer-literature-ilya-changed-a-lot/

original tweet

2026-03-23 12:15:39
Rachel Reid discusses Shane Hollander’s autism in “Heated Rivalry”, and Hudson Williams’s strong understanding of this trait (via @PinkNews) “When I wrote the first book, it was more about his overall personality. But I’ve talked about before, how in the time between Heated Rivalry and The Long Game, my own child was getting assessed for things like ADHD and autism. I learned a lot over those few years, and then I was like, ‘Oh yeah, Shane is autistic.’ By the time I wrote The Long Game, I was like, yeah, that’s what it is.” “Jacob knew right away. Jacob told me I think in the very first Zoom call, he was like, ‘Well, Shane’s autistic.’ He’s definitely approached the character that way. And when I talked to Hudson about it, he said the same thing. He was like, ‘Well, Shane is very autistic.’ We’d have a discussion about something where Connor might say something about Shane and I heard Hudson be like, ‘No, no, [he’s] doing that because he’s autistic.’ And I was like, ‘Yes, yes.’” “I think because of that, which unfortunately is also common of people with autism, the things that Shane does and says get misunderstood, sometimes by characters in the book and sometimes by readers who are like, ‘Well, that was a terrible thing to say or do.’ And it’s like, well, he’s not great at reading social cues. He’s got a lot of internalised anxiety. He’s very particular about certain things. Things bother him that don’t bother other people. I’m glad that everybody involved with the character understands that and I didn’t even have to explain it. Jacob got it. Hudson got it. I think most readers get it too. Lately I’ve been trying to just make it clear that, yes, he’s an autistic character.” 🔗 https://www.thepinknews.com/2026/03/23/heated-rivalrys-rachel-reid-on-developing-shane-and-ilya-their-group-chat-and-queer-literature-ilya-changed-a-lot/

original tweet

2026-03-23 12:10:29
Rachel Reid talks about her friendship with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie to @PinkNews “They’re funny together. They tease me sometimes and I kind of tease them too, but I just really like them. I’m really grateful that they auditioned. I’m grateful that they got the parts and that they want to talk to me. And it’s lovely to hear them talk about the books and stuff in interviews. It’s nice that they mention me at all. I think there’s a lot of authors [who] wouldn’t get mentioned by actors or the director or anybody. But this is a good group. It’s really nice.” “I think when I got to see the episode, I got to see the rough cuts of the episodes and they didn’t, any time I mentioned anything [in our group chat], they would just get really mad at me and jealous! They were just like, ‘Shut up, Rachel!’” 🔗 https://www.thepinknews.com/2026/03/23/heated-rivalrys-rachel-reid-on-developing-shane-and-ilya-their-group-chat-and-queer-literature-ilya-changed-a-lot/

original tweet

2026-03-23 12:10:29
Rachel Reid talks about her friendship with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie to @PinkNews “They’re funny together. They tease me sometimes and I kind of tease them too, but I just really like them. I’m really grateful that they auditioned. I’m grateful that they got the parts and that they want to talk to me. And it’s lovely to hear them talk about the books and stuff in interviews. It’s nice that they mention me at all. I think there’s a lot of authors [who] wouldn’t get mentioned by actors or the director or anybody. But this is a good group. It’s really nice.” “I think when I got to see the episode, I got to see the rough cuts of the episodes and they didn’t, any time I mentioned anything [in our group chat], they would just get really mad at me and jealous! They were just like, ‘Shut up, Rachel!’” 🔗 https://www.thepinknews.com/2026/03/23/heated-rivalrys-rachel-reid-on-developing-shane-and-ilya-their-group-chat-and-queer-literature-ilya-changed-a-lot/

original tweet

2026-03-22 20:01:50
The behavior we continue to witness within this fandom is abhorrent and deeply disturbing. The racist, homophobic, and overall hateful comments and actions directed towards Hudson, Connor, the cast of ‘Heated Rivalry’ or to Hudson’s loved ones are entirely unacceptable and have no place on our page. We understand the hurt, anger, and disappointment many of you are feeling - we feel it too. This space is meant to be rooted in shared appreciation, not hostility. Please prioritize your mental health and step back when needed. It is also important to address the ongoing misinformation: no one at HWU hates or dislikes Connor. He is Hudson’s co-star, best friend and comfort person who has brought him so much happiness and love since they met and we are so grateful for him being safe place for Hud. These accusations are false, speculative, and harmful, and we will not tolerate the continued spread of this narrative. This is a queer-led space, run by queer individuals, supporting a queer story and its cast. At the end of the day, we were fans of ‘Heated Rivalry’ first - just like all of you. Supporting Hudson and Connor should never be framed as a competition. It is entirely possible, and frankly expected, to uplift both of them in their individual journeys without pitting them against each other. We have voluntarily dedicated a significant amount of our time to running this updates account and organizing giveaways to make things accessible for his fans and we will continue to protect our peace and our boundaries from harassment, misinformation, and hate in whatever way we judge is necessary to prevent us from deactivating our Twitter/X account. We have every right to do so. Our intention has always been to foster a safe, respectful, and welcoming environment - not only for the fandom, but for Hudson and anyone close to him who may come across this space. We do not wish to amplify hate by giving it unnecessary attention, but we are aware of the concerns regarding certain accounts. Please know that efforts are being made behind the scenes to address these issues. We encourage you to continue reporting harmful accounts, participating in reporting-only groupchats, contributing to accurate community notes, and refraining from direct engagement. What brought us all together was our shared love for this story and its characters. Let’s not lose sight of that. Please continue to support Hudson and Connor with the respect, kindness, and decency they deserve. We sincerely hope this will be the last statement we need to make. Our purpose as an account has always been to share updates and celebrate Hudson’s career - not to moderate or manage fandom behavior. While we will always speak up when it comes to crimes involving our page - we are only responsible for what WE post. Our focus remains on supporting Hudson, rather than continually addressing every conflict within the community. If our approach does not align with your expectations, you are free to disengage rather than resort to harassment.

A brand new theatre production of ‘Yaga’ is returning to the stage at the Tarragon Theatre (Toronto, ON) in 2027! Written by Kat Sandler and directed by Jill Harper, the production will run from May 11 to June 6, 2027. Cast to be announced. 🔗 Get your tickets here: https://purchase.tarragontheatre.com/EventAvailability?EventId=12401

2026-03-21 15:27:19
“Mr Nobody Against Putin” co-director Pavel Talankin spoke to a fan about meeting Hudson Williams at the #Oscars (via yorozanov) “He’s very big - although he looks slim in photos we took on my phone, in reality he’s very tall and broad-shouldered. He’s very kind. Before the ceremony he was with his team but still came over to talk. I told him he looks bigger (in a good way) than in photos. He said it’s no problem for him to gain or lose about 6 kg when needed. Then after the Oscars I went up to him, we talked. He asked about the film and stuff like that. He’s a good guy. Very simple. No star ego - doesn’t hide from people.”

original tweet

2026-03-21 15:27:19
“Mr Nobody Against Putin” co-director Pavel Talankin spoke to a fan about meeting Hudson Williams at the #Oscars (via yorozanov) “He’s very big - although he looks slim in photos we took on my phone, in reality he’s very tall and broad-shouldered. He’s very kind. Before the ceremony he was with his team but still came over to talk. I told him he looks bigger (in a good way) than in photos. He said it’s no problem for him to gain or lose about 6 kg when needed. Then after the Oscars I went up to him, we talked. He asked about the film and stuff like that. He’s a good guy. Very simple. No star ego - doesn’t hide from people.”

original tweet

2026-03-21 15:27:19
“Mr Nobody Against Putin” co-director Pavel Talankin spoke to a fan about meeting Hudson Williams at the #Oscars (via yorozanov) “He’s very big - although he looks slim in photos we took on my phone, in reality he’s very tall and broad-shouldered. He’s very kind. Before the ceremony he was with his team but still came over to talk. I told him he looks bigger (in a good way) than in photos. He said it’s no problem for him to gain or lose about 6 kg when needed. Then after the Oscars I went up to him, we talked. He asked about the film and stuff like that. He’s a good guy. Very simple. No star ego - doesn’t hide from people.”

2026-03-19 22:48:37
Hudson Williams was the #1 most-influential male celebrity at the 2026 #Oscars with a Media Impact Value of $3.76 Million for Balenciaga and $2.4 Million for Bulgari partnerships, for a total value of $6.16 Million (via @THR) “The Launchmetrics MIV algorithm assigns a monetary value to every post, social media interaction and editorial story to determine a brand’s influence and earned engagement.” 🔗 https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/lifestyle/style/who-won-oscars-red-carpet-which-brand-should-thank-zendaya-1236541032/

2026-03-19 21:29:31
Jenny Mac, script supervisor on ‘Heated Rivalry’, joins the Loon Lovers Live podcast to talk behind the scenes and share insights from her experience working on the show with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie. “Those two [Hudson and Connor] are so exceptionally funny and weird. I honestly don’t know where Jacob found them. I think another planet.” “The subtlety in which he [Hudson] brought the autism in his character to form was so well done … He really nailed that aspect of his character.” 🔗 Watch more here: https://youtu.be/KUHTZY353pQ

original tweet

2026-03-18 21:20:44
Jacob Tierney on writing season 2 of “Heated Rivalry” and how Hudson Williams surprised him during filming (via skytv) “The boys are excited, I’m excited [to do it]. It’s going to be fun to see what else we can unearth that feels real, sexy, funny and truthful. I’m going to do what I did in the first place, which is trust myself and trust the source material, and trust my instincts towards channeling it.”

2026-03-17 20:16:29
Hudson Williams’s character in ‘The Altruists’ (called Duncan Rheingans‑Yoo) was the co-founder of Modulo Capital, a Crypto trading fund that received a $400 Million investment from FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried right before the cryptocurrency exchange collapsed. The series is based on the collapse of FTX. Whilst Duncan isn’t accused of wrongdoing, investigators scrutinised the investment due to its timing and the fact that Modulo operated out of the same Bahamas resort complex where Sam and other FTX staff lived. Hudson Williams was booked for the role pre-“Heated Rivalry” and filmed his scenes last year.

original tweet

2026-03-17 20:16:29
Hudson Williams’s character in ‘The Altruists’ (called Duncan Rheingans‑Yoo) was the co-founder of Modulo Capital, a Crypto trading fund that received a $400 Million investment from FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried right before the cryptocurrency exchange collapsed. The series is based on the collapse of FTX. Whilst Duncan isn’t accused of wrongdoing, investigators scrutinised the investment due to its timing and the fact that Modulo operated out of the same Bahamas resort complex where Sam and other FTX staff lived. Hudson Williams was booked for the role pre-“Heated Rivalry” and filmed his scenes last year.

original tweet

The series is based on the collapse of FTX. Whilst Duncan isn’t accused of wrongdoing, investigators scrutinised the investment due to its timing and the fact that Modulo operated out of the same Bahamas resort complex where Sam and other FTX staff lived. Hudson Williams was booked for the role pre-“Heated Rivalry” and filmed his scenes last year.

2026-03-17 19:20:00
As previously known, Hudson Williams star in Netflix’s limited series ‘The Alturists’. It’s now been revealed that he will play Duncan Rheingans‑Yoo. Jennifer Grey, Terry Chen, Elizabeth Adams, Hannah Galway and William Mapother were also cast in the show. It is co-showrun and executive produced by Graham Moore and Jacqueline Hoyt. ‘The Altruists’ tells the story of two hyper-smart, ambitious young idealists (portrayed by Anthony Boyle and Julia Garner) who tried to remake the global financial system in the blink of an eye – before they were accused of stealing $8 billion and became Gen Z’s own Bonnie & Clyde. The cast series regulars includes Eugene Young, Madison Hu, Karan Soni, Matt Rife, Alex Lawther, and Naomi Okada. The recurring cast includes: Maddie Hasson, Marianna Phung, Paul Reiser, and Robin Weigert. 🔗 https://deadline.com/2026/03/hudson-williams-jennifer-grey-cast-the-altruists-1236757124/

original tweet

2026-03-17 19:20:00
As previously known, Hudson Williams star in Netflix’s limited series ‘The Alturists’. It’s now been revealed that he will play Duncan Rheingans‑Yoo. Jennifer Grey, Terry Chen, Elizabeth Adams, Hannah Galway and William Mapother were also cast in the show. It is co-showrun and executive produced by Graham Moore and Jacqueline Hoyt. ‘The Altruists’ tells the story of two hyper-smart, ambitious young idealists (portrayed by Anthony Boyle and Julia Garner) who tried to remake the global financial system in the blink of an eye – before they were accused of stealing $8 billion and became Gen Z’s own Bonnie & Clyde. The cast series regulars includes Eugene Young, Madison Hu, Karan Soni, Matt Rife, Alex Lawther, and Naomi Okada. The recurring cast includes: Maddie Hasson, Marianna Phung, Paul Reiser, and Robin Weigert. 🔗 https://deadline.com/2026/03/hudson-williams-jennifer-grey-cast-the-altruists-1236757124/

original tweet

2026-03-17 19:20:00
As previously known, Hudson Williams star in Netflix’s limited series ‘The Alturists’. It’s now been revealed that he will play Duncan Rheingans‑Yoo. Jennifer Grey, Terry Chen, Elizabeth Adams, Hannah Galway and William Mapother were also cast in the show. It is co-showrun and executive produced by Graham Moore and Jacqueline Hoyt. ‘The Altruists’ tells the story of two hyper-smart, ambitious young idealists (portrayed by Anthony Boyle and Julia Garner) who tried to remake the global financial system in the blink of an eye – before they were accused of stealing $8 billion and became Gen Z’s own Bonnie & Clyde. The cast series regulars includes Eugene Young, Madison Hu, Karan Soni, Matt Rife, Alex Lawther, and Naomi Okada. The recurring cast includes: Maddie Hasson, Marianna Phung, Paul Reiser, and Robin Weigert. 🔗 https://deadline.com/2026/03/hudson-williams-jennifer-grey-cast-the-altruists-1236757124/

Hudson Williams made @TheCut’s Best #Oscars Red-Carpet Looks! “As the undisputed internet “It” boy, he can do no wrong right now, which gives him free rein to swing big on the red carpet. But he still looks sharp as hell in this Balenciaga suit and a slutty little Bulgari Serpentini, so best dressed it is! Bonus points for being on trend with the brooch.” 🔗 See the full list here: https://www.thecut.com/article/oscars-2026-best-dressed-worst-dressed-fashion-looks.html

original tweet

2026-03-16 18:36:31
“Hudson Williams doesn't just draw crowds while out in the wild — he also had Hollywood's elite lining up for a chance to chat with him. The TV heartthrob was spotted holding court throughout the night with everyone from ‘One Battle’ star Chase Infiniti and F1's Damson Idris to ‘Hamnet’ stars Mescal and Joe Alwyn.” “Commercial breaks often caught Williams surrounded by a crowd or smiling for selfies. In one endearing moment, the actor was spotted heading to the bar after the emotional and extended segment honoring many of the Hollywood stars who died in the past year. While wiping tears away, he told a friend, ‘I don't think I can ever sit through an in memoriam again.’” - @EW’s commentary on Hudson Williams attending the #Oscars 🔗 https://ew.com/oscars-2026-everything-you-didnt-see-on-tv-at-the-academy-awards-11927157

original tweet

march 9 – march 15

2026-03-15

2026-03-15 23:57:15
Mens groomer Aika Flores talks about Hudson Williams’ ‘heartthrob glow’ and the full #Oscars skin care routine (via @glamourmag) “The key to a ‘heartthrob glow’ is hydration and healthy habits that begin well before the ‘Heated Rivalry’ star got ready for ceremony. ‘He's pretty good about it,’ she says of Williams. ‘He drinks a lot of water … I can't say he gets much sleep these days but you can’t tell.’ Of course, she can always resort to threats. ‘I'm like, every time you lose one hour sleep, you get one pore,’ she jokes, knowing full well that Williams hates the look of pores and ‘doesn't want to see 'em.’” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.glamour.com/story/how-hudson-williams-skincare-artist-achieved-his-heartthrob-glow-for-the-oscars

original tweet

2026-03-15 23:57:15

2026-03-14 21:35:17
Hudson Williams attending the Balenciaga FW26 Paris Fashion Show generated $1.2 Million in Earned Media Value for the brand (via Lefty Data and Karla Otto) For comparison, the entire Dsquared2 show in January generated $4.2 Million in EMV. As a reminder: Earned Media Value is a metric that assigns a monetary value to organic exposure, such as social media engagement. It calculates what it would have cost to achieve the same reach through paid advertising. 🔗 More information here: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/pfw-aw26-inside-global-spectacle-drove-404m-emv-karla-otto-ltd-rohie

original tweet

2026-03-14 15:30:45
The owner of the brand who made Hudson Williams’s CAA-Pre Oscar’s Party outfit (Viviano) via Instagram “What we try to do with our menswear is bring a sense of softness and femininity into it, to make it a bit more playful and expressive. (…) I’m personally a big fan of Heated Rivalry, so seeing him wear our piece honestly meant a lot to me. In a way it reminds me of the message behind that show — being honest with yourself and trusting your own instincts is what matters the most.” 🔗 https://www.instagram.com/p/DV3jlW_E0CV/?igsh=NTc4MTIwNjQ2YQ==

original tweet

2026-03-14 15:30:45
The owner of the brand who made Hudson Williams’s CAA-Pre Oscar’s Party outfit (Viviano) via Instagram “What we try to do with our menswear is bring a sense of softness and femininity into it, to make it a bit more playful and expressive. (…) I’m personally a big fan of Heated Rivalry, so seeing him wear our piece honestly meant a lot to me. In a way it reminds me of the message behind that show — being honest with yourself and trusting your own instincts is what matters the most.” 🔗 https://www.instagram.com/p/DV3jlW_E0CV/?igsh=NTc4MTIwNjQ2YQ==

2026-03-14 07:09:13
Hi, I’m sorry for quoting your tweet - I’ve already deleted it. I had seen several shady and mean comments on my timeline about people edging his pictures, and since you don’t follow us and we had also edged the pictures because we were excited, I assumed your tweet might have been referring to us too. That said, it was never my intention for anyone to attack you but to stop the shading and chaos. We were simply excited fans sharing the moment, and again I’m sorry about the situation!

original tweet

2026-03-14 07:09:13
Hi, I’m sorry for quoting your tweet - I’ve already deleted it. I had seen several shady and mean comments on my timeline about people edging his pictures, and since you don’t follow us and we had also edged the pictures because we were excited, I assumed your tweet might have been referring to us too. That said, it was never my intention for anyone to attack you but to stop the shading and chaos. We were simply excited fans sharing the moment, and again I’m sorry about the situation!

original tweet

2026-03-14 07:09:13
Hi, I’m sorry for quoting your tweet - I’ve already deleted it. I had seen several shady and mean comments on my timeline about people edging his pictures, and since you don’t follow us and we had also edged the pictures because we were excited, I assumed your tweet might have been referring to us too. That said, it was never my intention for anyone to attack you but to stop the shading and chaos. We were simply excited fans sharing the moment, and again I’m sorry about the situation!

original tweet

2026-03-14 06:23:13
@bratzsunwoo Next time, we simply won’t say anything in advance. It may be better that way, since situations like this often end up being blamed on us, even when we’re just trying to share the excitement as fans.

2026-03-13

2026-03-13 23:41:04
Shane Hollander’s autism isn’t the focus of ‘Heated Rivalry’ and why that matters (via @into_tweets) “Hollander’s character has many facets: He’s a hockey player, half Japanese, a gay man, highly competitive, and inescapably earnest. By highlighting these other dimensions, the show allows his autism to be just another facet—a part of the whole that makes up Shane Hollander.” [N]ormalizing autism in the media is essential for acceptance. “Making autism an everyday, normal part of the human experience is key to the world treating us as people who deserve to be living our lives, among our loved ones—not people to be pitied or looked down on, but as everyday people. Media representation, either coming directly from or with input from the autistic community, is a tool to combat fear of autistic people with acceptance,” says Noor Pervez, community engagement coordinator at the Autistic Self-Advocacy Network (ASAN). “I loved that the show featured someone with a successful family life, social life, and love life. There was acceptance—not pushing it to the background or making fun, but acknowledgment in a way that felt loving and supportive. Here’s someone with autism who might be socially awkward at times or struggle to make eye contact, but it’s still fine to those who care about him. He gets to be successful and be a regular person. I thought that was huge for the neurodivergent community: We can know someone is autism-coded without it being ‘a portrayal of autism,’” says Kate Hanselman, PMHNP. “Many stereotypes paint autistic people as either uninterested in or incapable of romantic relationships. It’s powerful to see a show that portrays an autistic man as capable of both desiring and being desired,” says Isabelle Mathewes, a researcher in the psychology department at the University of Virginia and autism advocate. “Shane Hollander’s character shows that autism doesn’t have to be loud to be meaningful. By making it just one part of who he is, ‘Heated Rivalry’ offers a refreshing model of acceptance—one where autistic people can simply exist, thrive, and be loved exactly as they are.” 🔗 Read the full article: https://www.intomore.com/culture/heated-rivalry-is-quietly-changing-how-autism-looks-on-screen-and-fans-are-taking-notice/

original tweet

2026-03-13 23:41:04

By highlighting these other dimensions, the show allows his autism to be just another facet—a part of the whole that makes up Shane Hollander.” [N]ormalizing autism in the media is essential for acceptance. “Making autism an everyday, normal part of the human experience is key to the world treating us as people who deserve to be living our lives, among our loved ones—not people to be pitied or looked down on, but as everyday people. Media representation, either coming directly from or with input from the autistic community, is a tool to combat fear of autistic people with acceptance,” says Noor Pervez, community engagement coordinator at the Autistic Self-Advocacy Network (ASAN). “I loved that the show featured someone with a successful family life, social life, and love life. There was acceptance—not pushing it to the background or making fun, but acknowledgment in a way that felt loving and supportive. Here’s someone with autism who might be socially awkward at times or struggle to make eye contact, but it’s still fine to those who care about him. He gets to be successful and be a regular person. I thought that was huge for the neurodivergent community: We can know someone is autism-coded without it being ‘a portrayal of autism,’” says Kate Hanselman, PMHNP. “Many stereotypes paint autistic people as either uninterested in or incapable of romantic relationships. It’s powerful to see a show that portrays an autistic man as capable of both desiring and being desired,” says Isabelle Mathewes, a researcher in the psychology department at the University of Virginia and autism advocate. “Shane Hollander’s character shows that autism doesn’t have to be loud to be meaningful. By making it just one part of who he is, ‘Heated Rivalry’ offers a refreshing model of acceptance—one where autistic people can simply exist, thrive, and be loved exactly as they are.” 🔗 Read the full article: https://www.intomore.com/culture/heated-rivalry-is-quietly-changing-how-autism-looks-on-screen-and-fans-are-taking-notice/

2026-03-13 20:17:00
Anastasia Walker on Hudson Williams being into fashion and how she approaches his looks throughout the awards season (via @THR) “As stylists, we are also working and coordinating with agents and particular brand deals that they’ve arranged also may come into play. And sometimes there is a perception that if they are aligned with a brand, the brand will just be dressing them. I try to make sure I’m building good relationships with those teams as well, so that I’m able to insert a direction that we feel is right for that particular client. It might involve sending different mood boards back and forth vs. what was originally presented, so that I’m advocating for this particular vision or idea that we want to convey. Thankfully, the teams we’ve had for brand partnerships have been so amazing and lovely and collaborative in that way. So you’ll see some things this weekend that represent that. But we do put together some different things. We had a recent event where Hudson attended a Gold House party in New York, and Prabal Gurung was the host of that, so of course Hudson wore Prabal for that. We did play a bit with his suit, turning a belt into a neck sash and then using an earring on it so it looked like a brooch. He really likes to have fun with fashion, and that was reflective of that.” 🔗 Read more: https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/lifestyle/style/hudson-williams-ethan-hawke-rose-byrne-stylists-awards-1236529061/

original tweet

2026-03-13 20:17:00

And sometimes there is a perception that if they are aligned with a brand, the brand will just be dressing them. I try to make sure I’m building good relationships with those teams as well, so that I’m able to insert a direction that we feel is right for that particular client. It might involve sending different mood boards back and forth vs. what was originally presented, so that I’m advocating for this particular vision or idea that we want to convey. Thankfully, the teams we’ve had for brand partnerships have been so amazing and lovely and collaborative in that way. So you’ll see some things this weekend that represent that. But we do put together some different things. We had a recent event where Hudson attended a Gold House party in New York, and Prabal Gurung was the host of that, so of course Hudson wore Prabal for that. We did play a bit with his suit, turning a belt into a neck sash and then using an earring on it so it looked like a brooch. And sometimes there is a perception that if they are aligned with a brand, the brand will just be dressing them. I try to make sure I’m building good relationships with those teams as well, so that I’m able to insert a direction that we feel is right for that particular client. It might involve sending different mood boards back and forth vs. what was originally presented, so that I’m advocating for this particular vision or idea that we want to convey. original tweet

2026-03-13 20:12:36
Anastasia Walker, Hudson Williams’ stylist, spoke with @THR about his upcoming attendance at the #Oscars this Sunday, March 15. THR: How are you approaching his look for Sunday night? AW: Nothing is bigger than the Oscars, so the main focus is that obviously it’s extremely formal. But we’re making sure we’re bridging the gap between that formality while also having a modern take on it. The clients I have who are attending are not your classic guys; they are really fashion-forward. THR: What will be going through your mind when Hudson has finished dressing and is heading out to the red carpet? AW: Honestly I’m still holding my breath at that point. I don’t breathe until I’ve seen it on camera and also know that the photos are good. Our agents are also on it; they’re watching the livestream and also looking at the photos coming in. Meanwhile we’ve also checked the weather in advance—it’s supposed to be 88 or 90 degrees when the carpet opens, so I’ll be thinking about sweat. I’m always worried about sweat, but I’m also worried about how the jacket falls or whether someone will be on hand to make adjustments before he steps onto the carpet. So I’m always on edge until the very end. 🔗 Full interview: https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/lifestyle/style/hudson-williams-ethan-hawke-rose-byrne-stylists-awards-1236529061/

original tweet

2026-03-13 20:12:36

But we’re making sure we’re bridging the gap between that formality while also having a modern take on it. The clients I have who are attending are not your classic guys; they are really fashion-forward. THR: What will be going through your mind when Hudson has finished dressing and is heading out to the red carpet? AW: Honestly I’m still holding my breath at that point. I don’t breathe until I’ve seen it on camera and also know that the photos are good. Our agents are also on it; they’re watching the livestream and also looking at the photos coming in. Meanwhile we’ve also checked the weather in advance—it’s supposed to be 88 or 90 degrees when the carpet opens, so I’ll be thinking about sweat. I’m always worried about sweat, but I’m also worried about how the jacket falls or whether someone will be on hand to make adjustments before he steps onto the carpet. original tweet

2026-03-12 18:24:29
For the oscars, we have to be smart and give him engagement 💗 The three tags we will be using is: #HudsonWilliamsAtTheOscars2026 #Oscars  #Oscars2026 Don’t use the first one before sunday! We are gonna use it in all tweets we do about it and I really need you guys to stop pressuring us about adding links. We CAN’T add links to every tweet for three reasons: 1. We got shadowbanned once because of that; 2. Our account might get suspended; 3. It’s CRAZY BUSY! Sometimes we see a content and we spend 5 minutes searching for the source cause people just drop it on timeline and we care about giving credit. We won’t have time to do it all. I can’t promise a tweet with all the links on Sunday cause I don’t know how crazy will be but it will come somehow. And I would be very happy to see more people using the tags since I didn’t see last event 🥺. I know it’s annoying so here’s a tip to make it easier: Go to Settings > General > Keyboard > Text Replacement and choose the easiest way to replace the tags! Thank you for the patience and support 🌷

original tweet

2026-03-12 18:24:29
For the oscars, we have to be smart and give him engagement 💗 The three tags we will be using is: #HudsonWilliamsAtTheOscars2026 #Oscars  #Oscars2026 Don’t use the first one before sunday! We are gonna use it in all tweets we do about it and I really need you guys to stop pressuring us about adding links. We CAN’T add links to every tweet for three reasons: 1. We got shadowbanned once because of that; 2. Our account might get suspended; 3. It’s CRAZY BUSY! Sometimes we see a content and we spend 5 minutes searching for the source cause people just drop it on timeline and we care about giving credit. We won’t have time to do it all. I can’t promise a tweet with all the links on Sunday cause I don’t know how crazy will be but it will come somehow. And I would be very happy to see more people using the tags since I didn’t see last event 🥺. I know it’s annoying so here’s a tip to make it easier: Go to Settings > General > Keyboard > Text Replacement and choose the easiest way to replace the tags! Thank you for the patience and support 🌷

original tweet

2026-03-12 18:24:29
For the oscars, we have to be smart and give him engagement 💗 The three tags we will be using is: #HudsonWilliamsAtTheOscars2026 #Oscars  #Oscars2026 Don’t use the first one before sunday! We are gonna use it in all tweets we do about it and I really need you guys to stop pressuring us about adding links. We CAN’T add links to every tweet for three reasons: 1. We got shadowbanned once because of that; 2. Our account might get suspended; 3. It’s CRAZY BUSY! Sometimes we see a content and we spend 5 minutes searching for the source cause people just drop it on timeline and we care about giving credit. We won’t have time to do it all. I can’t promise a tweet with all the links on Sunday cause I don’t know how crazy will be but it will come somehow. And I would be very happy to see more people using the tags since I didn’t see last event 🥺. I know it’s annoying so here’s a tip to make it easier: Go to Settings > General > Keyboard > Text Replacement and choose the easiest way to replace the tags! Thank you for the patience and support 🌷

(via ottawatourism) THE WINNER RECEIVES: ✈️ Round-trip flights for (2) to Ottawa 🔑 Two-night stay for (2) on the Gold Floor at Fairmount Château Laurier 🐺 One-night stay for (2) at Parc Omega chalet, with outdoor fire pit ✨ $50 gift card to Hello Dolly Pastries 🍝 Dinner for (2) at Dreamland Pasta Cafe 🦆 (2) passes to the Canadian Museum of Nature  🔗 For more information & rules to enter: https://www.instagram.com/p/DVOv2oLldVo/?img_index=1

USA Women’s hockey Gold medalist Megan Keller talks about meeting Hudson Williams at SNL (via @WomensHealthMag) “[Hudson and Connor] were like two of the nicest people I’ve ever met. Just to also see like them behind the scenes, and what they do on a day-to-day basis, it was so incredible to watch. I was so nervous and they made it so easy.”

Rachel Reid tells the Loon Call podcast that Hudson Williams shared his SNL dressing room with her so she could watch the show from backstage She also reveals that his appearance at dinner beforehand was a complete surprise to not only herself, but to Jacob and Brendan too “He came directly from the airport to dinner to surprise me. I turned around and was like ‘Fuck. Off’.” 🔗 https://youtu.be/MCbtZzajjVg

2026-03-08 21:19:23
According to a fan at tonight’s Waterstones “Heated Rivalry” event, Rachel Reid said that a TV tie in book cover for “Heated Rivalry” is confirmed and “in the works, [coming] sooner rather than later” (via hudconenjoyer) They are also continuing conversations about Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie re-recording the audio book: “We’d all like it. Things are being discussed. Our stars became superstars so might be tricky. It might happen.”

original tweet

Since he’s not in Paris anymore, here it is: Hudson Williams yesterday was at the balcony with Aika Flores (his hairstylist and friend) signing autographs and sending them as little paper planes and then he shouted that he ran out of paper for them to be careful with the heads and threw a clementine. via sofianealarcon & _anais_louise

2026-03-07 17:22:16
New data from Launchmetrics reveals that Hudson Williams contributed to 54% ($8.8M) of Dsquared2’s total Media Impact Value from their Milan Fashion Week FW26 show in January ($19.5M total) His Instagram post was also the #1 top placement related to the show, generating $440k in Media Impact Value, almost 12x more value than the brands usual content “MIV is Launchmetrics’ proprietary metric used to measure the impact of brand mentions across various Voices and channels. It assigns a monetary value to exposure from social media, online publications and other media sources, helping brands assess the effectiveness of their PR and marketing efforts.” 🔗 https://nomannersmagazine.com/heritage-brands-asian-stars-and-heated-rivalry-boost-media-impact-value-at-mens-and-couture-fashion-week/

original tweet

2026-03-07 17:08:06
Madison Beer tells @ZachSangShow that she binged “Heated Rivalry” in one day and is listening to the soundtrack “All the songs from ‘Heated Rivalry’, like, ‘My Moon My Man’. (…) It’s a really beautiful story, like made me sob my eyes out. (…) It was pitched to me as a [sexy hockey show], but it’s so much more than that. It’s a beautiful story.”

2026-03-07 02:17:46
Since everyone is talking about Hudson’s love for Shane, it’s time to bring back this legendary crumb for the new fans! At the Toronto premiere Q&A, Hudson admitted he actually lurked on Twitter and Reddit to see what people thought of Shane before filming. He literally studied the discourse to get the character right not only through the books but valuing fan’s opinions. We love a dedicated king. 🥺

original tweet

2026-03-07 01:21:44
Lesa Hannah (@LesaHannah), journalist that interviewed the talented Zinka Tuminski and Joan Chell - the makeup and hair stylists behind Heated Rivalry - shared with HWU the Hollander mood board they created with Hudson while developing Shane’s hairstyle. “Although my hair isn't long enough to do the longer styles I still think we can influence the boyish charm with a short sides, messy textured bangs cut.” 🥺 Read her interview here: https://www.theglobeandmail.com/life/style/fashion-and-beauty/article-heated-rivalry-hair-makeup-artists/

2026-03-06 20:43:40

original tweet

2026-03-06 18:40:39
Chell also added freckles to Williams, which are a feature of his character in the Game Changers book series that the show is based on. In her work on Anne of Green Gables, she had hand-drawn freckles onto the main character, dotting them on one by one – it was gruelling work. Fortunately, she had come across a better option while working on upcoming horror film Forbidden Fruits: a freckle kit from Korean brand Maykoo. Applied with a stamp, the product comes with the option of three shades. Chell changed up the colour depending on the lighting of the scene. “If it was really bright, I wanted them a little more subdued,” she said. In dark, moody rooms, she would kick it up a notch. 🔗 https://www.theglobeandmail.com/life/style/fashion-and-beauty/article-heated-rivalry-hair-makeup-artists/

original tweet

2026-03-06 18:40:39
Chell also added freckles to Williams, which are a feature of his character in the Game Changers book series that the show is based on. In her work on Anne of Green Gables, she had hand-drawn freckles onto the main character, dotting them on one by one – it was gruelling work. Fortunately, she had come across a better option while working on upcoming horror film Forbidden Fruits: a freckle kit from Korean brand Maykoo. Applied with a stamp, the product comes with the option of three shades. Chell changed up the colour depending on the lighting of the scene. “If it was really bright, I wanted them a little more subdued,” she said. In dark, moody rooms, she would kick it up a notch. 🔗 https://www.theglobeandmail.com/life/style/fashion-and-beauty/article-heated-rivalry-hair-makeup-artists/

original tweet

2026-03-06 18:40:39
Chell also added freckles to Williams, which are a feature of his character in the Game Changers book series that the show is based on. In her work on Anne of Green Gables, she had hand-drawn freckles onto the main character, dotting them on one by one – it was gruelling work. Fortunately, she had come across a better option while working on upcoming horror film Forbidden Fruits: a freckle kit from Korean brand Maykoo. Applied with a stamp, the product comes with the option of three shades. Chell changed up the colour depending on the lighting of the scene. “If it was really bright, I wanted them a little more subdued,” she said. In dark, moody rooms, she would kick it up a notch. 🔗 https://www.theglobeandmail.com/life/style/fashion-and-beauty/article-heated-rivalry-hair-makeup-artists/

original tweet

2026-03-06 18:37:20
Williams had lots of ideas for his character’s hair, which he and Zuminski mapped out with photos. “He was very into it, and we talked about it at length for every single year,” she said. “We had an idea that he’d have these soft bangs, and then in the in-between years, it was slightly to the side. And then when he was older, just right off his face.” To indicate the passing of time, Zuminski kept Williams and Storrie’s hair pushed forward during Shane and Ilya’s rookie years to make them look younger. As they aged, she moved the hair off their faces to look more groomed and styled, especially during scenes where they were wearing suits. Read the full article: https://www.theglobeandmail.com/life/style/fashion-and-beauty/article-heated-rivalry-hair-makeup-artists/

original tweet

2026-03-06 18:37:20
Williams had lots of ideas for his character’s hair, which he and Zuminski mapped out with photos. “He was very into it, and we talked about it at length for every single year,” she said. “We had an idea that he’d have these soft bangs, and then in the in-between years, it was slightly to the side. And then when he was older, just right off his face.” To indicate the passing of time, Zuminski kept Williams and Storrie’s hair pushed forward during Shane and Ilya’s rookie years to make them look younger. As they aged, she moved the hair off their faces to look more groomed and styled, especially during scenes where they were wearing suits. Read the full article: https://www.theglobeandmail.com/life/style/fashion-and-beauty/article-heated-rivalry-hair-makeup-artists/

original tweet

2026-03-06 18:37:20
Williams had lots of ideas for his character’s hair, which he and Zuminski mapped out with photos. “He was very into it, and we talked about it at length for every single year,” she said. “We had an idea that he’d have these soft bangs, and then in the in-between years, it was slightly to the side. And then when he was older, just right off his face.” To indicate the passing of time, Zuminski kept Williams and Storrie’s hair pushed forward during Shane and Ilya’s rookie years to make them look younger. As they aged, she moved the hair off their faces to look more groomed and styled, especially during scenes where they were wearing suits. Hudson Williams’ short film “Sheepskin” directed by Ethan Wingrove will be screened at NFFTY (National Film Festival for Talented Youth) in Seattle on March 28 at 11 AM. We don’t have any information if he’s attending. HWU will be there ☝🏻 You can purchase your tickets for the screening here: https://nffty2026.eventive.org/films/6994eb218ef9f813e6f4eb3d (Choose Evergreen Screen) “Sheepskin” trailer below 👇🏻 3 young criminals are being interrogated to reveal the location of their mysterious boss. But what they don't reveal, is even more deadly.

2026-03-05 16:59:29
Today is the 37th Annual GLAAD Media Awards! “Heated Rivalry” is nominated for ‘Outstanding New TV Series’, with cast and creatives confirmed to attend. We do not have any information if Hudson Williams will be attending. The ceremony itself will not be broadcast live but Gaye Magazine will be livestreaming the red carpet starting at 4:30pm PST / 7:30pm EST on TikTok, Instagram Live, Twitch and YouTube. The ceremony will premiere on Hulu on March 21st.

original tweet

original tweet

2026-03-05 16:33:24
Jackson Parrell, the cinematographer and director of photography of ‘Heated Rivalry’, talks about Hudson Williams’ performance during Shane’s various advertising bits. (via @itsnicethat) “One of my favourite shots is a slow push in on Shane when he’s doing an ad. They spritz his hair, he cracks a can, his mother comes in and says some shit to him, and then we start rolling, pulling back out – and he’s just grinning. That’s very much Hudson’s personal humour really fitting the character in that moment, he really played that bit well.” 🔗 Full interview: https://www.itsnicethat.com/features/heated-rivalry-jackson-parrell-cinematography-film-spotlight-050326

original tweet

2026-03-05 16:33:24
Jackson Parrell, the cinematographer and director of photography of ‘Heated Rivalry’, talks about Hudson Williams’ performance during Shane’s various advertising bits. (via @itsnicethat) “One of my favourite shots is a slow push in on Shane when he’s doing an ad. They spritz his hair, he cracks a can, his mother comes in and says some shit to him, and then we start rolling, pulling back out – and he’s just grinning. That’s very much Hudson’s personal humour really fitting the character in that moment, he really played that bit well.” 🔗 Full interview: https://www.itsnicethat.com/features/heated-rivalry-jackson-parrell-cinematography-film-spotlight-050326

original tweet

2026-03-05 16:33:24

(via @itsnicethat) “One of my favourite shots is a slow push in on Shane when he’s doing an ad. They spritz his hair, he cracks a can, his mother comes in and says some shit to him, and then we start rolling, pulling back out – and he’s just grinning. That’s very much Hudson’s personal humour really fitting the character in that moment, he really played that bit well.” 🔗 Full interview: https://www.itsnicethat.com/features/heated-rivalry-jackson-parrell-cinematography-film-spotlight-050326

2026-03-04 18:51:17
Callan Potter (Hayden Pike) on working with Hudson Williams on “Heated Rivalry” (via @digitaljournal) “Hudson is amazing! He is so fun and so charismatic. I love that guy! He is such a lovely dude, he’s so funny, and great to talk to. (…) We had a great time on set. It’s so awesome to watch him get the recognition that he deserves because he worked his ass off on that show. I’m so happy for him. I’m objectively so proud and happy for his success.” 🔗 https://www.digitaljournal.com/entertainment/callan-potter-talks-about-starring-in-heated-rivalry-series-on-max/article

original tweet

2026-03-04 18:51:17
Callan Potter (Hayden Pike) on working with Hudson Williams on “Heated Rivalry” (via @digitaljournal) “Hudson is amazing! He is so fun and so charismatic. I love that guy! He is such a lovely dude, he’s so funny, and great to talk to. (…) We had a great time on set. It’s so awesome to watch him get the recognition that he deserves because he worked his ass off on that show. I’m so happy for him. I’m objectively so proud and happy for his success.” 🔗 https://www.digitaljournal.com/entertainment/callan-potter-talks-about-starring-in-heated-rivalry-series-on-max/article

Hudson Williams’ short film “Rancid” will be screened at Hollywood ShortsFest in Los Angeles on April 19 at 7:35 PM. You can claim your free tickets for the screening here: https://filmfreeway.com/HOLLYWOODSHORTSFEST/tickets?utm_source=ig&utm_medium=social&utm_content=link_in_bio (Choose “Episode 1 - Breathless” - the last option!) 2026-03-03 19:14:34
A fan recounts meeting Hudson Williams recently to @CHEK_News “When I was waiting for my person, I noticed him come off the plane, but I’m like, ‘No, that couldn’t be him.’ (...) Then I watched one of the other host parents go and shake his hand. (...) I had this sticker on my phone of the two characters. So I showed him the sticker and I said, ‘Oh I make beaded earrings about your characters’. He’s the sweetest, he’s so down to earth.” “And then it kind of hit me, I was like, ‘Oh, can I take a picture?’ And he says, ‘Of course.’ And I go to take a picture and he says, ‘Wait, don’t you want to get a picture of both of us?’ And his driver took a picture of both of us.” 🔗 https://cheknews.ca/hes-the-sweetest-heated-rivalry-star-hudson-williams-spotted-in-victoria-1308960/

original tweet

2026-03-03 19:14:34
A fan recounts meeting Hudson Williams recently to @CHEK_News “When I was waiting for my person, I noticed him come off the plane, but I’m like, ‘No, that couldn’t be him.’ (...) Then I watched one of the other host parents go and shake his hand. (...) I had this sticker on my phone of the two characters. So I showed him the sticker and I said, ‘Oh I make beaded earrings about your characters’. He’s the sweetest, he’s so down to earth.” “And then it kind of hit me, I was like, ‘Oh, can I take a picture?’ And he says, ‘Of course.’ And I go to take a picture and he says, ‘Wait, don’t you want to get a picture of both of us?’ And his driver took a picture of both of us.” 🔗 https://cheknews.ca/hes-the-sweetest-heated-rivalry-star-hudson-williams-spotted-in-victoria-1308960/

Then I watched one of the other host parents go and shake his hand. (...) I had this sticker on my phone of the two characters. So I showed him the sticker and I said, ‘Oh I make beaded earrings about your characters’. He’s the sweetest, he’s so down to earth.” “And then it kind of hit me, I was like, ‘Oh, can I take a picture?’ And he says, ‘Of course.’ And I go to take a picture and he says, ‘Wait, don’t you want to get a picture of both of us?’ And his driver took a picture of both of us.” 🔗 https://cheknews.ca/hes-the-sweetest-heated-rivalry-star-hudson-williams-spotted-in-victoria-1308960/

2026-03-03 11:25:56
Rachel Reid discusses writing ‘Unrivaled’ and Shane Hollander’s hero arc across the three Hollanov books “I do think that the three books, as a trilogy, it’s Shane who has like the hero’s arc. I think, even in The Long Game, it’s Shane. As much as the book focuses on Ilya, Shane is the one with that arc, and I do think that continues into this one [Unrivaled]. I do think Shane sort of is the main character of these three books. It’s pretty even, I think, in [Unrivaled], because it’s really about them as a unit. They’re not apart very much. It’s probably a little more even than the other ones.” “It’s been fun kind of writing them — especially Shane, having to face the fact that he’s not going to be playing hockey forever. You know, his body’s gonna start to not do what it used to do.” “I think having them at an age where maybe they’re starting to think about the future, beyond hockey, and their lives together, is nice. It’s a very different vibe from writing them in their teens and early 20s, much like anyone’s thinking about different things, usually, when they’re into their 30s.” 🔗 Watch the full interview here: https://www.out.com/books/rachel-reid-unrivaled

original tweet

2026-03-03 11:25:56
Rachel Reid discusses writing ‘Unrivaled’ and Shane Hollander’s hero arc across the three Hollanov books “I do think that the three books, as a trilogy, it’s Shane who has like the hero’s arc. I think, even in The Long Game, it’s Shane. As much as the book focuses on Ilya, Shane is the one with that arc, and I do think that continues into this one [Unrivaled]. I do think Shane sort of is the main character of these three books. It’s pretty even, I think, in [Unrivaled], because it’s really about them as a unit. They’re not apart very much. It’s probably a little more even than the other ones.” “It’s been fun kind of writing them — especially Shane, having to face the fact that he’s not going to be playing hockey forever. You know, his body’s gonna start to not do what it used to do.” “I think having them at an age where maybe they’re starting to think about the future, beyond hockey, and their lives together, is nice. It’s a very different vibe from writing them in their teens and early 20s, much like anyone’s thinking about different things, usually, when they’re into their 30s.” 🔗 Watch the full interview here: https://www.out.com/books/rachel-reid-unrivaled
